meta.title: Push Manifesto — A way to do creativity
meta.description: The Push Manifesto is a set of principles and values that guide organisations and individuals in the pursuit of innovation and progress.

hero.lede: A set of principles for innovation and progress — vision, collaboration, inclusive behaviour, and a pragmatic, evidence-based mindset. It prioritises the journey, using waypoints over milestones, targeting shared value for everyone involved.

manifesto.heading: Prioritise the journey.
manifesto.body1: Push Manifesto is about vision, collaboration, inclusive behaviours, determination, communication, governance, and learning — and above all it prioritises the journey: using waypoints over iterations and milestones, balancing the desire for fit-for-purpose with shared value outcomes for users and stakeholders.
manifesto.body2: It feeds the Maturity Model and an evidence-based mindset, supporting the scientific approach — daring to explore the latent space, with a pragmatic world-view.

principles.heading: The building blocks of a push.

push.label: Push
push.description: A logical unit of output that has a beginning, middle and end. ‘End’ generally represents an outcome, doesn’t need to immediately progress to the next, includes all work items, has value, and has an agreement of ‘Done’ — everybody gets something.

ambiguity.label: Ambiguity & Cognitive Bias
ambiguity.description: These kill projects. Offer high readability, clear mental models, support diverse audiences, adopt simple conventions, reduce complexity, take the least-cost route, seek clarity and always, always question. Take an agnostic approach.

risk.label: Risk
risk.description: Again, kills projects. You don’t get to space by crossing your fingers — continuously cite and counter risks. Pretty sure Babbage, Boole and Bayes knew Murphy.

checkins.label: Check-ins, Waypoints & Reviews
checkins.description: Whilst stand-ups are useful, prefer a low-ceremony, less-noisy follow-up on work items — top and bottom of the week, honesty and shared understanding. Your child’s report card should not be a surprise, by virtue of care and involvement.

roadblocks.label: Identify & Remove Road Blocks
roadblocks.description: Early identification is key, with an inclusive approach taken.

sharedValue.label: Creating Shared Value
sharedValue.description: An inclusive and humble approach, in recognition that your project — whilst having value and benefits — contributes to wider goals.

miseEnPlace.label: Mise en place
miseEnPlace.description: Don’t start a project without your mise en place done. Get those vegetables chopped.

workItems.label: Work Items
workItems.description: Work items, tasks and cards transform Assets. Link the work item to the asset. **Don’t** deposit results back into the task. Knowledge management is first-class.

gettingItWrong.label: Getting it Wrong
gettingItWrong.description: Being learned, critical thinking and taking a scientific approach will always help you climb those mountains.

goFindOut.label: Go Find Out
goFindOut.description: Engage. Engage early, and enough — even if to make friends. Get out of the chair and go find out. Do the work, and don’t repeat the obvious.

rollEm.label: Know when to Roll ’Em
rollEm.description: Manage your work effort, dial in your approach and don’t push your luck. Only kick a push if the conditions are favourable. If it can go wrong it will — Murphy’s law applies.

hypothesis.label: Hypothesis
hypothesis.description: Every great _idea_ starts with a test strategy. It doesn’t exist if it can’t be tested.

cta.headingLead: Get out of the chair and
cta.lede: Read the long-form thinking behind the manifesto on the blog, or dig into the source on GitHub.

footer.tagline: Push Manifesto — a way to do creativity.
